title: Fix drag-and-drop in save diagram so only the thumbnail preview image is draggable

requirements.text: In the AttackPathToolbar (and any related save diagram UI), fix drag-and-drop so that only the thumbnail preview image of a saved diagram is draggable onto the canvas. The surrounding element (labels, borders, background, buttons) must not be draggable. Apply draggable={true} and drag event handlers exclusively to the thumbnail element, and set draggable={false} on the parent container and all sibling elements.

quotes: I'm referring to dragging a thumbnail preview of the diagram itself
fix the save diagram function and I should only drag the image icon to canvas

acceptanceCriteria: Dragging the thumbnail preview image of a diagram initiates a drag event and allows it to be dropped onto the canvas.
Dragging the label, border, background, or any non-thumbnail part of the diagram element does not initiate a drag.
The drag-and-drop canvas drop behavior works correctly when the thumbnail is dragged onto the AttackPathCanvas.
No regression in existing toolbar icon drag-and-drop functionality.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Changing how diagrams are saved or loaded from the backend.
Redesigning the toolbar or sidebar layout.
Adding new drag-and-drop targets beyond the existing canvas.
